_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of models for examining cyber threats (e.g. cyber kill chain, MITRE ATT&CK).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI-specific attack taxonomy, including adversarial examples, model evasion, model extraction, model inversion, membership inference, data poisoning, and prompt injection.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-T-002 (RM, cosine: 0.512), AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.455).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.562). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [att, chain, ck, cyber, examining, kill] vs WIDAI-only terms [adversarial, ai, attack, data, evasion, examples] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.848 (P=0.847, R=0.848). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (81.1%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of models for examining cyber threats (e.g. cyber kill chain, MITRE AT" and "Knowledge of AI-specific attack taxonomy, including adversarial examples, model". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.539 ≈ STS 0.562 (gap 0.023). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [knowledge] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.2%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [att, chain, ck, cyber, examining, kill]. WIDAI-only: [adversarial, ai, attack, data, evasion, examples].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 81.1% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
